tests.description: init() adds a THREE.InstancedMesh to the scene
InstancedMesh uses a 3D BufferGeometry (not Points)
mesh uses ShaderMaterial with vertex and fragment shaders
same seed produces same primitive type selection (deterministic)
different seeds can produce different primitive types (vertex count differs for at least one pair)
at least 3 distinct primitive types are possible across seeds
InstancedMesh has instanceMatrix attribute with finite values
instance count matches InstancedMesh.count property
maxInstances=200 produces fewer instances than maxInstances=1200
instance count never exceeds configured maxInstances
density parameter scales effective instance count (higher density = more instances)
draw() does not throw with valid FrameState
draw() updates shader uniforms (uTime reflects frame elapsed time)
bass energy updates uBassEnergy uniform on draw
treble energy updates uTrebleEnergy uniform on draw
bass drives macro position displacement: instanceMatrix changes with bass energy
treble drives per-instance rotation jitter or scale pulse: instanceMatrix changes with treble energy
instanceMatrix.needsUpdate is set to true after draw()
cleanup() removes InstancedMesh from scene and disposes geometry/material
setOpacity updates uOpacity uniform for crossfade
paletteHue uniform reflects param on draw
motionAmplitude uniform reflects param on draw
boundary values (density=0, density=1, structureComplexity=0/1) do not throw
draw does not throw with edge-case params (zero bass, zero treble, no pointer)
density=0 still produces at least 24 instances (minimum floor)
instance positions have non-zero Z-depth (3D volume, not flat)
instance transforms span all three axes (not coplanar)
material is transparent with depthWrite disabled
shader includes noise3d declarations (curl3 or snoise symbols)
shader declares all required shared uniforms
instanceMatrix evolves over time (non-static animation)
no localStorage or cookie access during init/draw operations
all instanceMatrix values remain finite after draw with extreme audio params
multiple draw calls do not throw or leak objects into the scene
geometry uses small primitives (bounding box radius < 0.1)

imports: import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';

helpers.defaultParams:
const defaultParams: VisualParams = {
  paletteHue: 180,
  paletteSaturation: 0.5,
  cadence: 0.7,
  density: 0.6,
  motionAmplitude: 1.0,
  pointerDisturbance: 0,
  bassEnergy: 0,
  trebleEnergy: 0,
  curveSoftness: 0.5,
  structureComplexity: 0.5,
  noiseFrequency: 1.0,
  radialScale: 1.0,
  twistStrength: 1.0,
  fieldSpread: 1.0,
};

tests.description: QualityProfile includes maxInstances field
low tier has maxInstances=200, medium=600, high=1200
ModeManager accepts MicroGeometry as a fifth mode entry
